evals.prompt: Run an adversarial verification on my last commit
evals.expected_output: Full CoV review with decomposed claims, adversarial questions, independent verification, and evidence-based report
assertions: Identifies the verification domain (Code) via auto-detection
Gathers artifacts via git diff
Establishes ground truth sources (tests, type system, spec)
Decomposes code changes into individual verifiable claims
Generates adversarial counter-questions for each claim
Traces actual code paths to verify each claim independently
Produces a structured report with PASS/FAIL results, file:line references, and confidence scores
Checks for project docs (TODO.md, SPEC.md, PLAN.md) and proposes updates if relevant

evals.prompt: CoV check this function that adds items to a list while iterating over it
evals.expected_output: Detection of concurrent modification bug with specific evidence and fix
assertions: Identifies the concurrent modification as a verifiable claim
Generates adversarial question about nested calls modifying the list
Reports a FAIL with confidence >= 80
Provides the exact file and line number of the issue
Suggests a concrete fix (e.g., iterate over a copy or use a separate buffer)

evals.prompt: Verify this code — it looks fine to me, just a simple getter
evals.expected_output: Skeptical analysis that does not trust surface-level simplicity
assertions: Does not accept 'looks correct' as evidence of correctness
Decomposes even simple code into verifiable claims
Checks for initialization order, null safety, and boundary conditions
Applies the anti-pattern rule: 'simple code has simple bugs'
Produces a structured verification report regardless of code complexity

evals.prompt: Verify the PLAN.md against the SPEC.md — check if the implementation plan covers all requirements
evals.expected_output: Architecture domain verification detecting spec drift and missing requirements coverage
assertions: Identifies the verification domain as Architecture
Reads both PLAN.md and SPEC.md as artifacts
Uses SPEC.md as ground truth source
Decomposes PLAN.md steps into claims about requirement coverage
Detects any SPEC requirements without a corresponding PLAN step
Reports spec drift with severity and confidence scores

evals.prompt: Adversarial check on this database migration that adds a new column
evals.expected_output: Data domain verification checking schema consistency, defaults, and backward compatibility
assertions: Identifies the verification domain as Data
Reads the migration file and the ORM model as artifacts
Checks for NOT NULL without default value on existing table
Verifies the ORM model matches the migration schema
Checks for rollback migration existence
Evaluates backward compatibility with existing queries

evals.prompt: Verify this agent's analysis report — it claims our API handles 10k req/s based on the benchmarks
evals.expected_output: Analysis domain verification checking for hallucinated facts and stale references
assertions: Identifies the verification domain as Analysis
Reads the analysis report and attempts to locate cited benchmark data
Checks if performance claims are traceable to actual benchmark results
Detects any hallucinated or unsupported claims
Checks for confirmation bias (only positive results cited)
Reports findings with confidence and specific source discrepancies

evals.prompt: Run adversarial verification on the last commit and update project docs with findings
evals.expected_output: Full CoV review followed by proposed project doc updates, waiting for user confirmation
assertions: Completes the full CoV verification protocol (Steps 0-4)
Scans for project files: TODO.md, SPEC.md, PLAN.md, CHANGELOG.md
Proposes specific additions to relevant project docs
Does NOT auto-write to any project files
Presents proposed updates in the structured format with file names and changes
Waits for explicit user confirmation before applying updates
